questions.text:
Complete the sentences about number systems. Use the items from the list. Some of the items in the list will not be used. You should only use an item once.

A B C D E F G
H W X Y Z 0 1
2 4 8 10 16 127 128
255 256

The binary number system is base [blank] . The smallest denary number that can be represented as an 8-bit binary number is [blank] . The largest denary number that can be represented as an 8-bit binary number is [blank] .
The hexadecimal number system is base [blank] . Each hexadecimal digit is equivalent to [blank] bits. The numbers 1 to 9 are used and the number 10 is represented by [blank] . The hexadecimal number system continues up to the number 15, which is represented by [blank] .

answers.scoring_rule: 1 mark for each correct item (in bold)

marking_points.text: The binary number system is base 2.
The smallest denary number that can be represented an 8-bit binary number is 0.
The largest denary number that can be represented an 8-bit binary number is 255.
The hexadecimal number system is base 16.
Each hexadecimal digit is equivalent to 4 bits.
The numbers 1 to 9 are used and the number 10 is represented by A.
The hexadecimal number system continues up to the number 15, which is represented by F.

questions.text:
Two 8-bit binary numbers are given.
Add the two 8-bit binary numbers using binary addition.
Give your answer in binary. Show all your working.

  1 0 0 1 1 0 1 1
+ 0 0 0 1 0 0 1 1

answers.scoring_rule: 1 mark for correct working, 1 mark for each correct nibble

marking_points.text: 1 1 1 (correct working/carry bits shown)
1 0 1 0 (correct upper nibble of answer)
1 1 1 0 (correct lower nibble of answer)

questions.text:
Binary addition can result in overflow.
Tick (✓) one box to show the correct definition of overflow in binary addition.

A The answer has created a negative number that cannot be represented in binary addition.
B The answer is the result of a logical shift that cannot be performed in binary addition.
C The answer is too large to represent in the number of bits available.
D The answer is too small to represent in the number of bits available.

rows: The answer has created a negative number that cannot be represented in binary addition.
The answer is the result of a logical shift that cannot be performed in binary addition.
The answer is too large to represent in the number of bits available.
The answer is too small to represent in the number of bits available.

marking_points.text: C

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer.
State what is meant by the colour depth of an image.

marking_points.text: Number of bits used to represent each/a/one colour
Number of colours that can be represented

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer.
Describe the relationship between the resolution of the image and the file size of the image.

marking_points.text: The higher the resolution the more pixels
The higher the resolution the more bits to store
The higher the resolution the larger the file size

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer. The artist compresses the image file before uploading it to their website. Users can download the image file from the website to print as a poster. The image file is compressed using lossless compression.
Explain the reasons why the artist chose lossless compression instead of lossy compression to compress this image file.

marking_points.text: Lossless will retain all parts of the image // lossless does not delete any data // The artist does not want any of the image to be lost when downloaded
Lossy will reduce the quality
… because the artist wants to provide the detailed image
Image may be small in file size anyway
… server has enough space to store the image
… download time is not excessive

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer. The artist compresses the image file before uploading it to their website. Users can download the image file from the website to print as a poster. The image file is compressed using lossless compression.
Identify one lossless method of compressing an image. Describe your chosen method.

Method
Description

answers.scoring_rule: 1 mark for naming, max 2 for describing, for example:

marking_points.text: Run-length encoding
Repeating pixels of one colour are identified // patterns are identified
… and indexed // put into a table // assigned a value
… as the number of repetitions and the colour code

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer. The artist emails the image file to their friends.
Explain how a file is broken down into packets and transmitted over a network.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Data is split into fixed size packets
The data is the payload
Each packet is given a header
… example of header data e.g. Destination IP, packet number, hop number
Each packet has a trailer
… example of trailer data e.g. error checking method
Router directs each packet towards its destination
Routes selects the most efficient path
Each packet can take a different path
Packets can arrive out of order
… after the last packet has arrived, they are reordered

questions.text:
An artist creates an image on a computer. The artist emails the image file to their friends. The packets can use many different methods of data transmission to travel.
Complete and annotate the diagram to show the difference between serial full-duplex transmission between two computers and parallel simplex transmission between two computers.

Serial full-duplex transmission:
Computer 1          Computer 2

Parallel simplex transmission:
Computer 1          Computer 2
visuals: Diagram showing two pairs of computers: one pair for serial full-duplex transmission and one pair for parallel simplex transmission, with space to draw and annotate the connections between them.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Parallel simplex having multiple wires and serial having one wire
Serial full-duplex going both ways at the same time
Parallel simplex only going one way

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU), memory and secondary storage. The computer has a Von Neumann architecture.
Circle three registers that are found in the Von Neumann architecture.

accumulator   arithmetic register   binary counter
binary register   control register   current data register
current instruction register   data counter   instruction counter
logical register   program counter   storage register

marking_points.text: Accumulator
Current instruction register
Program counter

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU), memory and secondary storage.
State the purpose of the CPU in the computer.

marking_points.text: Perform FDE cycle
Execute/process instructions

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU), memory and secondary storage.
Computer A has a 2.1GHz quad-core processor.
Computer B has a 2.5 GHz dual-core processor.
Computer C has a 5.2 GHz single-core processor.
Identify which computer is most likely to execute more instructions simultaneously. Justify your answer.

Computer
Justification

marking_points.text: Computer: A
It has 4 cores/processors
…each core will execute one instruction at a time
4 instructions can be executed simultaneously (others can only execute 1 or 2)

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU), memory and secondary storage.
Computer A has a 2.1GHz quad-core processor.
Computer B has a 2.5 GHz dual-core processor.
Computer C has a 5.2 GHz single-core processor.
Explain why Computer C can execute more instructions per second than Computer B.

marking_points.text: C has a higher clock speed // B has max 5 GHz
C can run 5.2 billion instructions/FDE cycles per second
B can only run 5 billion instructions/FDE cycles per second
Single core has less latency

questions.text:
A computer has a central processing unit (CPU), memory and secondary storage. All three computers have the same instruction set.
Define the term instruction set.

marking_points.text: a list of the (machine code) commands that can be processed by a CPU

questions.text:
An automated teller machine (ATM) is a device where a person inserts their bank card and can request to withdraw money. The ATM also allows the person to check how much money they have in their bank account.
A sensor is used to detect when a person stands within 1 metre of the ATM. When a person is detected, a welcome message is displayed on the screen.
Identify an appropriate sensor for the ATM to use to detect a person.

marking_points.text: Infra-red/proximity

questions.text:
An automated teller machine (ATM) is a device where a person inserts their bank card and can request to withdraw money. The ATM also allows the person to check how much money they have in their bank account.
A sensor is used to detect when a person stands within 1 metre of the ATM. When a person is detected, a welcome message is displayed on the screen.
Describe the role of the microprocessor in detecting the person.

marking_points.text: Receive digital data continually from the sensor
Uses sensor data to calculate distance to person
Compares calculated/sensor data to stored data/1m
… if data/comparison indicates person is within 1m, it sends signal to display welcome message on screen

questions.text:
An automated teller machine (ATM) is a device where a person inserts their bank card and can request to withdraw money. The ATM also allows the person to check how much money they have in their bank account.
Describe the features of the ATM that identify it as an embedded system.

marking_points.text: Dedicated function // special purpose device // not general purpose device
Dedicated hardware // by description, for example hardware is only used for ATM functions
Cannot be (easily) reprogrammed
Uses firmware
Contains microprocessor // does not have CPU

questions.text:
An automated teller machine (ATM) is a device where a person inserts their bank card and can request to withdraw money. The ATM also allows the person to check how much money they have in their bank account.
The ATM has a touch screen and a keypad. The ATM may need to be used by people who are unable to see.
Identify one other input device and one other output device that can be built into the ATM to help people who are unable to see.

Input
Output

marking_points.text: Input: Microphone
Input: Braille keyboard/pad
Output: Speaker

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. The computer has a network interface card (NIC) that has a media access control (MAC) address.
One purpose of the NIC is to provide the computer with a MAC address.
Describe the other purposes of the NIC.

marking_points.text: Transmit/send data to the network/devices // Receive data from the network/devices
To allow a physical connection
Convert network data so it can be understood by the computer
Convert computer data to be transmitted over the network
To be assigned an IP address

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. The computer has a network interface card (NIC) that has a media access control (MAC) address.
Identify three characteristics of a MAC address.

1
2
3

marking_points.text: (Represented) in hexadecimal
Numbers are separated by colons/hyphens
Six groups of digits
Sets of 2-digit/8-bit (hex) numbers // 48 bits // 12-digits
Contains manufacturer ID and unique device number
Static address // cannot be changed

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. The network allocates an internet protocol (IP) address to the computer.
Tick (✓) one box to identify a valid IPv4 address.

A 110:255:2:1
B 1.30.2FF.A9
C 3.162.74.3
D 8.0.257.6.8
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: C

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. The network allocates an internet protocol (IP) address to the computer.
Identify the device in a network that can automatically assign an IP address to a computer, each time the computer connects to the network.

marking_points.text: Router

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. A user accesses the world wide web using a web browser.
Define the term world wide web.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: A collection of web pages

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. A user accesses the world wide web using a web browser. The uniform resource locator (URL) for a website includes the protocol hypertext transfer protocol secure (HTTPS).
Explain how HTTPS makes the transmission of data secure.
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: Uses SSL/TLS
Encrypts data
… which stops the data being understood if intercepted
… using asymmetric encryption
… data is encrypted using the web server's public key
… data can only be decrypted by the web server's private key
Data transmitted to the server can only be decrypted by the server
Uses digital certificates

questions.text:
A computer is connected to a network. A user accesses the world wide web using a web browser. The uniform resource locator (URL) for a website includes the protocol hypertext transfer protocol secure (HTTPS). The protocol is one part of a URL.
Give the other two parts of a URL.

1
2
questions.topic_name: The Internet and the World Wide Web

marking_points.text: Domain name
Web page name // file name

questions.text:
A search engine uses artificial intelligence (AI) to identify the most relevant web pages for the user.
One characteristic of AI is the ability to learn.
Identify three other characteristics of AI.

1
2
3

marking_points.text: Collection of data
Rules for data
Ability to reason
Ability to adapt

questions.text:
A search engine uses artificial intelligence (AI) to identify the most relevant web pages for the user. The search engine uses machine learning.
Explain how machine learning is used by the search engine.

marking_points.text: Changes its data/rules
… according to feedback/results
If a result does not give a suitable response this is recorded
… next time the result is changed
… to provide more accurate/relevant results in future
Supervised/unsupervised learning

questions.text:
Programs can be written in a high-level language or a low-level language. Machine code is one type of low-level language.
Identify one other type of low-level language.
Identify the translator that is needed for the type of low-level language you have identified.

Type of low-level language
Translator
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Language: assembly
Translator: assembler

questions.text:
Programs can be written in a high-level language or a low-level language. A high-level language can be translated into a low-level language, using a compiler or an interpreter.
Describe the advantages of using an interpreter instead of a compiler during software development.
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Each statement is translated and executed before the next
Translator stops when an error is found
… can correct and continue from where error found // real time debugging when/where error found
... does not need to retranslate all the code again
Can test sections of the code
Can test without all the program code being functional/accurate/complete

questions.text:
Programs can be written in a high-level language or a low-level language. Integrated development environments (IDEs) provide translators.
Identify three other common functions of an IDE.

1
2
3
questions.topic_name: Types of Programming Language, Translators and IDEs

marking_points.text: Code editor
Run-time environment
Error diagnostics // by example
Auto-complete
Auto-correct
Prettyprint // syntax highlighting
Collapse/expand block
Auto-documentation
